title: Entity Registry (seed) — people -> companies -> roles
description: TEMPLATE, not data. The people->companies->roles single-source-of-truth the original instance never built as a standalone artifact (ARCHITECTURE.md §7) — so it is the FIRST artifact a new instance seeds. Mechanism only: every value below is a {company-slot} marker; the filled instance copy is `company_profile.entity_registry` in a manifest.json validated against tooling/config.schema.json. PEOPLE are MANDATORY (the registry's irreducible seed); COMPANIES are DERIVABLE from __Projects/ folder names, so the `companies` block is OPTIONAL — list a company only to record a role/alias a folder name alone can't carry.
x-seed-order: FIRST — seed before any content load; an unresolved person/company is the root cause of the recurring entity tangles the framework inverts.

people.description: MANDATORY seed. One row per known person (own-company staff + recurring external contacts). Fills company_profile.entity_registry.people. The entity-resolver C module resolves names/emails seen in email + meetings against this list; the focus-detector EXTENDS it per person via person_profile.focus.extra_entities (never overwrites it).

email.description: {person-email} — primary address; the key the email adapter resolves senders against.

company.description: {company-slot} — employing org. OPTIONAL: own-company staff omit it (use internal:true); fill for external contacts to bind them to a company in `companies`.

internal.description: True for own-company staff (matches company_profile.company.domain). Drives internal-vs-external sender classification.

companies.description: OPTIONAL — companies are DERIVABLE from __Projects/ folder names, so seed a row here ONLY to record something the folder name can't: a role label, an alias, or the canonical Overview path. Absent rows are still recognised from their folder. Has no manifest counterpart of its own; the resolver folds these into the project graph.

name.description: {company-slot} — canonical display name, matching the __Projects/ folder (minus any date prefix).

role.description: {company-role} — relationship/function, e.g. a supply-chain role or 'customer'/'research partner'.

aliases.description: Other names/spellings the resolver should fold into this entity.

overview.description: {overview-path} — forward-slash root-relative path to the company's Overview.md.

_examples.description: NOT part of the registry — illustration only. An instance DELETES this key and fills `people` (and optionally `companies`) for real.
